response.justification: The candidate lacks direct experience in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, or deep-learning workload analysis — core requirements for the Principal Performance Architect role. Their background is heavily focused on web, mobile, and full-stack software development with minimal exposure to hardware-software co-design, GPU architectures, or performance modeling of AI accelerators, making them a poor fit despite strong software engineering skills.
strengths: Extensive full-stack development experience across multiple languages and platforms
Demonstrated ability to build and maintain complex open-source systems (e.g., GISCollective)
Strong track record in performance-critical systems using DLang and asynchronous I/O
concerns: No evidence of SoC, IP, or AI accelerator modeling experience
Lacks documented work in deep learning optimization or GPU architecture analysis
Education is a BS in CS — insufficient without 8+ years of relevant hardware-focused experience
